marketFocus: Trump announced a 5-day halt on US strikes against Iranian energy facilities and signalled direct talks. Iran denied formal talks but acknowledged messages through mediators. WTI crashed $10.10 to $88.13/bbl, Brent -$12.25 to $99.94/bbl. Oil partially rebounded on reports of gas facility strikes in Isfahan.

US stocks rallied sharply on de-escalation: SPX +1.15%, NDX +1.22%, RUT +2.29%. DXY regained poise from 99.105 low. AUD Flash PMI collapsed to 47.0 Composite (prev 52.4) — largest single-month drop in 2 years. Japan Core CPI decelerated to 1.6% (exp 1.7%).

Flash PMIs due today across EUR, GBP, USD (09:30–14:45 FFT). RBNZ Breman: 'higher inflation near term, some growth impacts.' ECB Vujcic: 'must be vigilant facing stagflation risk.' BOJ Ueda: 'underlying inflation to accelerate moderately.'

USD.tone: De-Escalation Removes Risk-Off Bid — DXY Regaining Poise
USD.summary: USD is Neutral today as the Iran de-escalation narrative removes the risk-off safe-haven bid that drove the dollar higher on Monday. Trump's 5-day halt on energy strikes and the prospect of direct talks have reduced the October Fed hike repricing. DXY recovered from the 99.105 low overnight but lacks a clean directional catalyst. Fed Daly noted 'two possible paths' — the stagflation dilemma remains unresolved. Flash PMIs (14:45 FFT) are the key event today. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: Iran de-escalation removes risk-off bid — October Fed hike bets partially unwound
DXY recovered from 99.105 low but lacks clean directional catalyst
Fed Daly: 'two possible paths' — stagflation dilemma unresolved
Oil crash reduces inflationary pressure: WTI $88.13/bbl, Brent $99.94/bbl
Flash Manufacturing + Services PMI at 14:45 FFT — key catalyst today

EUR.tone: Energy Importer Headwind Persists — ECB Stagflation Warning
EUR.summary: EUR is Bearish today despite the oil crash. Gas facility strikes in Isfahan caused a partial oil rebound, and EUR remains an energy importer exposed to supply disruption risk. ECB's Vujcic warned: 'must be vigilant facing stagflation risk; if hikes needed, better to start small.' EUR/USD failed to sustain above 1.1600 and is back under pressure. German Flash PMI (09:30 FFT) and EZ Flash PMI (10:00 FFT) are the key events — a miss would reinforce the bearish case. Weekly Weak Bullish vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: ECB Vujcic: 'must be vigilant facing stagflation risk; if hikes needed, start small'
Gas facility strikes in Isfahan — partial oil rebound; EUR energy exposure persists
EUR/USD failed to sustain above 1.1600 — back under pressure
German Flash PMI at 09:30 FFT — miss would reinforce bearish case
EZ Flash PMI at 10:00 FFT — composite reading key for EUR direction today

GBP.tone: UK Geopolitical Risk Premium Persists — Flash PMIs Key
GBP.summary: GBP is Bearish today as the UK geopolitical risk premium from Monday persists. UK ministers confirmed the US is using UK bases for operations against Iran, and Iran's Araghchi warning that 'Starmer is putting British lives in danger' remains in the headlines. GBP/USD failed to sustain 1.3400 and is consolidating lower. UK Flash Manufacturing and Services PMIs at 09:30 FFT are the key events — a miss would accelerate the decline. Weekly Weak Bullish vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: UK geopolitical risk premium: US using UK bases for Iran operations
Iran's Araghchi: 'Starmer is putting British lives in danger' — still in headlines
GBP/USD failed to sustain 1.3400 — consolidating lower
UK Flash Manufacturing PMI at 09:30 FFT — key catalyst today
UK Flash Services PMI at 09:30 FFT — services sector health critical for BOE narrative

JPY.tone: USD/JPY Bounced — CPI Decelerated, Risk-On Returns
JPY.summary: JPY is Bearish today as the de-escalation risk-on environment reverses Monday's haven bid. USD/JPY bounced back from 158.00 as oil crashed and risk appetite returned. Japan's National Core CPI decelerated to 1.6% (exp 1.7%) and Headline CPI to 1.3% (exp 1.5%) — the data softens the urgency for BOJ normalization despite Ueda's hawkish tone. BOJ Ueda: 'underlying inflation to accelerate moderately; price trend to rise gradually.' Weekly Neutral vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: Risk-on return: USD/JPY bounced from 158.00 as oil crashed and equities rallied
Japan Core CPI 1.6% (exp 1.7%), Headline 1.3% (exp 1.5%) — deceleration
BOJ Ueda: 'underlying inflation to accelerate moderately' — hawkish but data softens urgency
New hawkish BOJ board members Asada and Sato confirmed — medium-term support
Haven demand fading as Iran de-escalation narrative takes hold

AUD.tone: Flash PMI Collapsed — Composite 47.0 (prev 52.4), Largest Drop in 2 Years
AUD.summary: AUD is Bearish today after the Australian Flash PMI collapsed to a Composite of 47.0 (prev 52.4) — the largest single-month drop in two years. Services PMI fell to 46.6 (prev 52.8), signalling contraction. The US FX Wrap noted: 'AUD & CAD lag given recent outperformance on energy independence.' AUD/USD is back below 0.7000. The RBA hike story is now competing with a sharp domestic slowdown signal. Gold and copper remain under pressure. Weekly Weak Bullish vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: Flash Composite PMI 47.0 (prev 52.4) — largest single-month drop in 2 years
Flash Services PMI 46.6 (prev 52.8) — contraction territory
US FX Wrap: 'AUD & CAD lag given recent outperformance on energy independence'
AUD/USD back below 0.7000 — post-RBA hike gains at risk
Gold and copper under pressure — commodity channel weakening

CAD.tone: Oil Crashed $10.10 on Iran De-Escalation — WTI $88.13, Brent $99.94
CAD.summary: CAD is Bearish today as oil crashed on Trump's Iran de-escalation announcement. WTI fell $10.10 to $88.13/bbl and Brent fell $12.25 to $99.94/bbl — the biggest single-day oil drop since the conflict began. The US FX Wrap explicitly noted 'AUD & CAD lag given recent outperformance on energy independence.' CAD's petro-currency advantage has been sharply reduced. Oil partially rebounded on Isfahan gas facility strikes but the trend reversal is significant. No major CAD events today. Weekly Weak Bullish vs Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: WTI crashed $10.10 to $88.13/bbl — biggest single-day drop since conflict began
Brent -$12.25 to $99.94/bbl — back below $100 psychological level
US FX Wrap: 'AUD & CAD lag given recent outperformance on energy independence'
Partial rebound on Isfahan gas facility strikes — but trend reversal significant
No major CAD events today — pure oil proxy; tracking de-escalation narrative

CHF.tone: Haven Demand Fading — SNB Intervention Resistance Still Present
CHF.summary: CHF is Neutral today as haven demand fades with the Iran de-escalation. Monday's extreme risk-off conditions that overrode SNB intervention resistance have eased. EUR/CHF is stabilizing as both EUR and CHF face opposing forces — EUR has energy import headwind, CHF has SNB intervention resistance. SNB Schlegel speaks today (time TBC) — any reiteration of the intervention language would cap CHF upside. Weekly Weak Bearish vs Daily Neutral = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: Haven demand fading as Iran de-escalation narrative takes hold
SNB intervention resistance still present — 'willingness to intervene has increased'
EUR/CHF stabilizing: EUR energy headwind vs CHF official resistance
SNB Schlegel speech today — intervention language reiteration would cap CHF
SNB Quarterly Bulletin (Wed) — next key event for CHF direction

NZD.tone: RBNZ Breman Soft — High-Beta Under Pressure, No Domestic Catalyst
NZD.summary: NZD is Bearish today as RBNZ Governor Breman delivered a cautious tone overnight: 'higher inflation near term, some growth impacts — watching for second-round effects.' The soft tone adds pressure to an already weak NZD. AUD/NZD dipped below 1.2000 briefly as both Antipodeans are under pressure, but NZD remains the weaker of the two given the absence of any rates support. The high-beta risk proxy nature of NZD means it underperforms in both risk-off (Monday) and mixed (Tuesday) environments. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION ALIGNED ✅
drivers: RBNZ Breman: 'higher inflation near term, some growth impacts' — soft tone
No domestic hawkish catalyst: no RBNZ meeting until April
High-beta risk proxy: underperforms in both risk-off and mixed environments
AUD/NZD dipped below 1.2000 — both Antipodeans under pressure
Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = High Conviction alignment confirmed
